raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a bike from XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) that arrived damaged right out of the box. I immediately took photos and sent them to XXXX, they said the bike was fine and they were just some scratches. When I started riding the bike I could tell it was not fine, it was making a squeaking noise. We got another new bike from them as well that doesn't have these issues. 

I asked XXXX to refund or replace the damaged bike. They agreed but said that I would have to package it for return. The box was damaged in the delivery/removal of the damaged bike, so I could not use it to re-package the bike. XXXX refused to send return packaging and said it would be my responsibility. 

I filed a dispute with both paypal and then amex, who both deferred to XXXX. I have a screen shot of Amex saying they have no control and they have to abide by merchant terms. But the merchant broke the agreement when they sent me a broken product. 

I placed my order with XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, the bike arrived XX/XX/XXXX and I emailed photos to XXXX as soon as I took it out of the box. The email was sent at XX/XX/XXXX at XXXXXXXX XXXX 

I suggested to XXXX they send me a replacement and I use the box from the replacement to ship back the defective one with a prepaid label they provide could then provide me with. 

When I could not resolve this with XXXX directly, I filed a dispute with XXXX, as I paid with my american express card through XXXX. XXXX treated it like a simple return/exchange and denied my dispute. 

American Express is refusing to stand behind me as a customer or follow the protections guaranteed under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666i ). I bought a product that arrived defective and not as described. The seller refused to provide any return packaging, the original box was destroyed when it was delivered, and sending it back would cost me hundreds of dollars out of pocket, which is completely unreasonable. 

AmEx originally opened a dispute but then closed it, saying, The merchant denied our dispute request. We suggest you reach out to them. We have exhausted our capability to assist you. That tells me AmEx is simply accepting whatever the merchant says instead of doing an independent investigation or applying the consumer protections required by law. 

The FCBA gives me the right to withhold payment when a product is defective, regardless of what the sellers policies say. AmExs refusal to act on that, even with clear evidence, is a violation of those protections and of my rights as a cardholder. 

I've been reasonable and willing to resolve this in several ways, but instead I'm being forced to either pay for return packaging materials, or pay for damaged goods.
